text:
Done. I implemented `intersect` in `/private/tmp/big-naQ4TJ/intersect.js` using the standard two-pointer sweep: at each step it takes the overlap `[max(starts), min(ends)]`, keeps it only when `start <= end` (so single-point touches like `[1,3] ∩ [3,5] = [3,3]` are included), then advances whichever interval ends first. Both inputs are already sorted, so the output comes out sorted in one pass, in O(|A| + |B|).

One caveat on verification: I couldn't run the automated test — my `node` test command is sitting behind a permission prompt that hasn't been approved, so I have not executed it. Tracing the required edge case by hand: for `[[1,3]]` and `[[3,5]]`, `start = max(1,3) = 3`, `end = min(3,5) = 3`, and `3 <= 3` pushes `[3,3]` — correct. The empty-list cases return `[]` because the loop never enters.

If you approve the `node` command (or tell me to proceed), I'll run the full case set — touching points, the classic interleaved example, empty inputs, one-covers-many, and no-overlap — and report the actual output.
